regime_justification: USDJPY holding ~159, 77 pips from 160 MOF watch-line; JGB at 2.78%, near 30-yr highs; BOJ minutes show growing hike pressure; Iran-peace headlines easing oil but yen not sustainably strengthening; two interventions in May exhausted; carry spread 186 bps still wide, vol still subdued – classic pre-intervention fragility.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY 159.23, 0.77 yen (77 pips) from 160 threshold; Japan intervened twice in early May; MOF exhausted 2/3 IMF intervention quota; JGB 2.78% at 30-yr peak; BOJ rate at 0.75% vs Fed 3.50-3.75%; VIX low at 16.76; oil pulled back on Iran peace talks but USDJPY hasn't broken decisively – all hallmarks of Stage 2 fragility.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY within 80 pips of intervention line; BOJ meeting June 15-16 (23 days out); oil easing on Iran optimism but yen not strengthening sustainably; JGB near 2.80%; if USDJPY tests 160 again, MOF has only one more 3-day intervention window under IMF rules.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Same fragility; no BOJ meeting until mid-June; if Iran talks stall oil rebounds and USDJPY retests 160; MOF intervention firepower nearly spent; applied unwind prior – would otherwise grade D given low vol and subdued MOVE.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: BOJ June 15-16 meeting inside window; hawkish signals (several members want hike 'without hesitation') vs. cautious majority; surprise hike to 1.0% would compress spread to ~275 bps, trigger repositioning; Iran peace uncertainty; scenario-weighted C.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Further out, if BOJ holds (base case) and Iran peace deal materializes, carry regime stabilizes; if hike occurs June 15-16, near-term unwind risk morphs into orderly tightening narrative; oil lower structurally helps JGB; 4-week window assumes resolution of June catalyst cluster – scenario-weighted D.

bottom_line: USDJPY at 159.23, 77 pips from intervention threshold; MOF has one 3-day window left under IMF rules; JGB at 2.78% (30-yr high), BOJ at 0.75%, spread 186 bps; oil down 1.1% today on Iran peace optimism but yen not reversing; VIX 16.76, MOVE 79.72, both subdued; BOJ June 15-16 meeting (3 weeks) is the next binary catalyst; grading C/C/C/D – Stage 2 Pre-Intervention fragility persists.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.23, 77 pips from 160; MOF intervened ~May 1 & May 6; has 1 more 3-day window under IMF rules (3 sessions/6 months to stay 'freely floating'); next test of 160 likely triggers last shot.

Next meeting Jun 15-16 (23 days). April minutes: 3 dissented for 1.0%; several want hike 'without hesitation' if inflation persists; majority cautious on Iran uncertainty. Market pricing ~40% hike to 1.0%; surprise would compress spread to ~275 bps, trigger unwind.

Currently 2.78%, touched 2.80% Mon May 18 (30-yr high); fiscal concerns (PM Takaichi urged supp budget), BOJ tightening expectations; if breaks decisively above 2.80%, signal of policy regime shift, margin pressure on carry.

Oil $99.50, down from ~$105 highs; Trump said 'final stages' of Iran talks but Supreme Leader hardened stance on uranium; Hormuz reopening not confirmed. If talks collapse, oil spikes back, yen weakness resumes, import-cost inflation reignites BOJ pressure.
MOVE 79.72, subdued; 52-wk high 115.02; rates vol not reflecting JGB surge yet; if fiscal concerns or BOJ tightening accelerates, MOVE spike would signal regime change in rates volatility.

narrative.p1: USDJPY closed 159.23 (+0.24%), holding 77 pips below the 160 MOF intervention line after two prior interventions in early May. Nikkei surged +3.1% on hopes Trump-Iran talks near conclusion, driving WTI down 1.1% to $99.50 (down ~6% over two sessions). JGB 10Y eased 1 bp to 2.78% after touching 30-yr high of 2.80% Monday; US 10Y up 3 bps to 4.59%. VIX dropped 3.9% to 16.76, MOVE down 2.2% to 79.72 – both complacent. Regime unchanged from yesterday: yellow (Stage 2 Pre-Intervention). Oil pullback on peace optimism provided brief yen relief but USDJPY not breaking lower decisively; carry spread still 181 bps, MOF down to final intervention window under IMF rules.
narrative.p2: The quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread computed at 181 bps (4.59 – 2.78), down from ~185 bps yesterday but still wide; JGB at 2.78% near 30-yr peak signals policy tightening expectations and fiscal stress; USDJPY pace +0.5% over 5 days, orderly but persistent. We are at Stage 2 Pre-Intervention: USDJPY within 80 pips of watch-line, JGB at multi-decade highs, MOF has exhausted 2/3 of IMF intervention quota, BOJ meeting 23 days out (Jun 15-16) where hawkish dissent (3 voted for 1.0% in April) could surprise. VIX/MOVE both low despite fragility – classic complacency. Grading 1w/2w C (Watchful): setup fragile, intervention risk elevated, BOJ catalyst inside 3-week window. Applied unwind prior to 2w – would otherwise grade D given subdued vol and lack of immediate catalyst; the prior tips ambiguous low-vol tape into C given proximity to 160 and exhausted MOF firepower. 3w also C (Jun BOJ meeting binary), 4w D (if BOJ holds and Iran peace materializes, carry stabilizes; if hike, near-term unwind becomes orderly tightening narrative).
narrative.p3: BTC $77,160 (-0.2% 1d, -4% 7d) is drifting despite equity risk-on rally; crypto liquidity sympathy muted. Nikkei +3.1% and oil pullback should be bullish for risk assets, yet BTC not participating – suggests crypto flows decoupled from traditional risk-on, possibly due to ongoing ETF outflows or positioning caution ahead of macro catalysts. For crypto holders: current setup is treacherous for levered longs; if USDJPY tests 160 again and MOF intervenes (triggering cross-asset vol spike), or if BOJ surprises hawkish June 15-16, expect sudden liquidity drain and BTC drawdown. The fact BTC is flat on a +3% Nikkei day is itself a warning: crypto not acting as risk-on proxy. Stay light, watch June 15-16 BOJ closely.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: No change 1w → 2w; both C. Fragility persists: USDJPY pinned near 160, MOF has one intervention window left, oil peace talks could stall and reverse. No BOJ meeting until Jun 15-16 (outside 2w window). Applied unwind prior to 2w grade – low VIX/MOVE and quiet tape would normally suggest D, but proximity to intervention threshold and exhausted MOF ammunition make the setup fragile. Data-driven grade is C; prior confirms.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Upgrade from C → C (no numeric change but reasoning shifts). Week 3 brings BOJ Jun 15-16 meeting into view. April minutes show 3 dissented for 1.0%, several members want hike 'without hesitation'; if inflation data (May CPI due early June) stays hot and Iran peace deal removes oil shock excuse, BOJ could surprise. Scenario-weighted: 40% chance of hike to 1.0% (market pricing), which compresses spread to ~275 bps and triggers unwind; 60% hold, carry stabilizes. Grade remains C but catalyst risk steps up.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Step down C → D. By week 4, BOJ Jun 15-16 decision is in the rear-view. Base case: BOJ holds at 0.75%, signals gradual path; Iran peace deal lowers oil structurally; USDJPY drifts back to 155-157 range without MOF intervention. If BOJ hiked week 3, the unwind becomes an orderly policy-tightening story rather than forced liquidation. Either path resolves the binary; 4-week scenario-weighted D assumes catalyst cluster resolves without systemic break.

watch_list: USDJPY tests 160.00 – triggers MOF's final 3-day intervention window; if breached cleanly, next stop 162-165 (carry resumes)
Iran peace talks – Trump said 'final stages' but Supreme Leader hardened uranium stance; if collapse, oil spikes back to $105+, yen resumes slide
BOJ June 15-16 meeting – watch May Japan CPI (due ~Jun 20, after meeting) and any pre-meeting Ueda/Tamura speeches; surprise hike to 1.0% is the 3-week binary
JGB 10Y above 2.80% – would signal fiscal/policy regime shift, margin pressure on carry, possible forced repositioning
VIX/MOVE spikes – if VIX >20 or MOVE >90, cross-asset vol cascade begins; currently both complacent (16.76 / 79.72)
